역대하 12:1.content: When the kingdom of Rehoboam was established and he was strong, he abandoned Yahweh’s law, and all Israel with him.

역대하 12:2.content: In the fifth year of king Rehoboam, Shishak king of Egypt came up against Jerusalem, because they had trespassed against Yahweh,

역대하 12:3.content: with twelve hundred chariots, and sixty thousand horsemen. The people were without number who came with him out of Egypt: the Lubim, the Sukkiim, and the Ethiopians.

역대하 12:4.content: He took the fortified cities which belonged to Judah, and came to Jerusalem.

역대하 12:5.content: Now Shemaiah the prophet came to Rehoboam, and to the princes of Judah, who were gathered together to Jerusalem because of Shishak, and said to them, “Yahweh says, ‘You have forsaken me, therefore I have also left you in the hand of Shishak.’ ”

역대하 12:6.content: Then the princes of Israel and the king humbled themselves; and they said, “Yahweh is righteous.”

역대하 12:7.content: When Yahweh saw that they humbled themselves, Yahweh’s word came to Shemaiah, saying, “They have humbled themselves. I will not destroy them; but I will grant them some deliverance, and my wrath won’t be poured out on Jerusalem by the hand of Shishak.

역대하 12:8.content: Nevertheless they will be his servants, that they may know my service, and the service of the kingdoms of the countries.”

역대하 12:9.content: So Shishak king of Egypt came up against Jerusalem and took away the treasures of Yahweh’s house and the treasures of the king’s house. He took it all away. He also took away the shields of gold which Solomon had made.

역대하 12:10.content: King Rehoboam made shields of bronze in their place, and committed them to the hands of the captains of the guard, who kept the door of the king’s house.

역대하 12:11.content: As often as the king entered into Yahweh’s house, the guard came and bore them, then brought them back into the guard room.

역대하 12:12.content: When he humbled himself, Yahweh’s wrath turned from him, so as not to destroy him altogether. Moreover, there were good things found in Judah.

역대하 12:13.content: So king Rehoboam strengthened himself in Jerusalem and reigned; for Rehoboam was forty-one years old when he began to reign, and he reigned seventeen years in Jerusalem, the city which Yahweh had chosen out of all the tribes of Israel to put his name there. His mother’s name was Naamah the Ammonitess.

역대하 12:14.content: He did that which was evil, because he didn’t set his heart to seek Yahweh.

역대하 12:15.content: Now the acts of Rehoboam, first and last, aren’t they written in the histories of Shemaiah the prophet and of Iddo the seer, in the genealogies? There were wars between Rehoboam and Jeroboam continually.

역대하 12:16.content: Rehoboam slept with his fathers, and was buried in David’s city; and Abijah his son reigned in his place.
